terms.definition: A massive, mud-brick temple structure unique to the Kerma culture. The Western Deffufa at Kerma was a major religious center.
Large burial mounds. The royal tumuli at Kerma contained not only the king but dozens of sacrificed individuals.
The blending of different religious or cultural traditions. The Napatan rulers syncretized Egyptian deities with Nubian traditions.
A sacred mountain near Napata believed by the Kushites to be the dwelling place of the god Amun.
An alphabetic script developed in Meroe, one of the oldest in Africa, used in both hieroglyphic and cursive forms.
The title for the queen mother in the Meroitic kingdom, often a powerful political and military figure.
The historic community of Jews in Ethiopia, whose origins are debated but may date back to the First Temple period.
Tall, upright stone slabs. Aksumite stelae were monumental funerary markers carved to resemble multi-storied buildings.
The ancient South Semitic language of Aksum, which remains the liturgical language of the Ethiopian Orthodox Church.
